Internal Tool — OneIM Practice

OneIM VB.NET Auditor

Automated static analysis, live benchmarking, and cascade dependency mapping for One Identity Manager VB.NET scripts and templates — catching production risks before they reach the synchronisation engine.

59 audit rules
5 risk categories
410 automated tests
8 graph algorithms
118 example files

The Problem It Solves

One Identity Manager stores business logic as VB.NET scripts and templates inside the database. These are edited through Designer's GUI, have no version control by default, and receive no automated code review before going live. A single bad template can:


Corrupt data for thousands of identities via a silent exception or wrong null check
Deadlock a sync thread indefinitely via circular template dependencies
Expose credentials hardcoded in connection strings that end up in audit logs
Make sync 100× slower via N+1 database queries inside loops
Break on deployment when a hardcoded business value doesn't match the target environment

How It Fits Into a Delivery Workflow

1
Export from Designer
System Debugger exports .vb files from the live system
2
Static Audit
59 rules scanned — seconds per file, no live connection needed
3
Live Benchmark
11 timed executions per template on a real system with regression detection
4
Cascade Analysis
Graph algorithms map dependency risks — critical path, deadlock candidates, single points of failure
5
HTML + JSON Report
Self-contained report for the team; JSON for CI pipeline gates

Tool Components

🔍

Static Code Audit

invoke_oneim_audit.ps1
Point at a folder of .vb files. Runs all 59 rules via regex and context-aware pattern matching. Produces a colour-coded HTML report with severity filters, per-file navigation, per-category risk scores, and a finding breakdown histogram. Also outputs JSON for CI pipelines.

Typical time for 20 templates: ~10 seconds

⏱

Live Benchmark

invoke_oneim_benchmark.ps1
Connects to a real OneIM system. Runs each template 10 times (1 warmup) and captures avg, p95, p99, standard deviation, GC pressure, and thread deltas. Detects regressions against a stored baseline — if a template that used to take 30ms now takes 60ms it is flagged automatically.

Typical time for 20 templates: 1–5 minutes

🕸

Cascade Dependency Graph

export_template_cascade_report.ps1
Maps the full upstream/downstream dependency web for a template. Runs 8 graph algorithms (see below) and renders findings in a visual HTML report with plain-English explanations. Companion JSON for further tooling.

Typical time: seconds (pure in-memory graph analysis)

📚

Reference Library Management

extract_custom_reference_files.ps1 and related scripts
Distinguishes custom code from out-of-the-box OneIM scripts. Custom candidates go through a formal review → promote / reject workflow with audit metadata. The auditor never flags vendor-shipped code as a violation.

🗃

Schema Snapshot & Diff

export_oneim_schema_snapshot.ps1 / compare_schema_snapshots.ps1
Point-in-time snapshot of tables, columns, and FK relationships. Comparing two snapshots detects schema drift — a column removed in PROD that still exists in DEV would silently break any template referencing it.

⚙

Rule Tooling

new_audit_rule.ps1 / validate_audit_rules.ps1 / export_audit_rules.ps1
Scaffold new rules from a template, validate the entire catalog for consistency (duplicate IDs, broken regexes, missing sources), export all rules to Markdown / CSV / JSON for documentation or review.

The 59 Audit Rules

17
Correctness
14
Security
13
Performance
8
Reliability
7
Concurrency
13
High severity
33
Medium severity
13
Low severity
CategoryRuleSeverity
CorrectnessOption Explicit should be enabledmedium
CorrectnessOption Strict should be enabledmedium
CorrectnessAvoid obvious late binding and Object-heavy codemedium
CorrectnessDispose disposable objects before losing scopemedium
CorrectnessUse explicit StringComparison for identifier-sensitive comparisonsmedium
CorrectnessReview GetTriggerValue-driven writes for Nothing or empty handlingmedium
CorrectnessUse Is Nothing / IsNot Nothing instead of = Nothing / <> Nothingmedium
CorrectnessDo not rethrow with Throw ex — bare Throw preserves the stack tracemedium
CorrectnessAvoid .ToLower()/.ToUpper() for string comparison — use OrdinalIgnoreCaselow
CorrectnessUse f.UidComparison() for UID_ columns instead of f.Comparison()medium
CorrectnessDirect = "" comparison on .GetValue("UID_...").String — use IsNullOrEmptylow
CorrectnessReview PutValue or ExecuteTemplate for sync-time side effectslow
CorrectnessCustom VI-KEY entries should use CCC-prefixed script nameslow
CorrectnessCustom VI-KEY entries should implement CCC-prefixed Function/Sub nameslow
CorrectnessVI_AE_ITDataFrom* called without comment-based column dependency declarationsmedium
CorrectnessAvoid Parse() — use TryParse() to prevent FormatExceptionmedium
CorrectnessHardcoded business value should use Connection.GetConfigParmValuelow
SecurityAvoid string-built SQL or filter expressionshigh
SecurityAvoid launching external processes from OneIM script logichigh
SecurityDo not use insecure deserializershigh
SecurityReview deprecated TLS or disabled certificate validationhigh
SecurityHardcoded credential or secret literalhigh
SecurityPassword embedded in a connection string literalhigh
SecurityLDAP filter built by string concatenation — injection riskhigh
SecurityProcess.Start() call detected — potential OS command injectionhigh
SecurityBroad exception handling should not hide failuresmedium
SecurityAvoid obsolete web request APIsmedium
SecurityReview outbound web requests with variable-driven URLsmedium
SecurityReview file I/O paths that appear variable-drivenmedium
SecurityReview XML parsing for insecure DTD or external entity behaviormedium
SecurityString.Format() with a variable format string — format injection riskmedium
PerformanceExpensive OneIM lookup (GetListFor / GetSingleProperty) inside a loopmedium
PerformanceIEntity.Create(Session) inside a loop — one SELECT per itemmedium
PerformanceConnection pooling disabled (Pooling=false) in ADO.NET connection stringmedium
PerformanceBuildSessionFactory() called per method invocation — should be cachedmedium
PerformanceIEntity.Save(Session) inside a loop — use UnitOfWork.Put() for bulk writesmedium
PerformanceSession.Source.GetList called with an empty WHERE filtermedium
PerformancePrefer Any over Count for emptiness checkslow
PerformanceAvoid repeated string concatenation in loopslow
PerformanceReview possible multiple enumeration of IEnumerablelow
PerformanceReview repeated object or database access inside loopslow
PerformanceObject allocation inside a loop bodylow
PerformanceQuery.SelectAll() loads every column — prefer Select() with needed columnslow
PerformanceMultiple TryGetSingleValue calls with same table/where clause — use TryGetlow
ReliabilityMultiValueProperty modified (.Add/.Remove) without PutValue writeback — changes silently losthigh
ReliabilityEmpty Catch block silently swallows exceptionsmedium
ReliabilityGetFk result used without IsEmpty guardmedium
ReliabilityUse HttpClient instead of obsolete HttpWebRequest / WebClientmedium
ReliabilityIDisposable declared with Dim instead of Using — resource may leakmedium
ReliabilityGetSingleValue() throws on no match — prefer TryGetSingleValue()medium
ReliabilityMultiValueProperty accessed by index without .Count bounds checkmedium
ReliabilityCType/DirectCast without a preceding TypeOf guardmedium
ConcurrencyNOLOCK hint may yield dirty reads under concurrent writeshigh
ConcurrencyBeginTransaction without visible Commit or Rollback in scopehigh
ConcurrencyNested transaction — BeginTransaction called twice before Commithigh
ConcurrencyTemplate write (PutValue/ExecuteTemplate) inside a dependency cyclehigh
ConcurrencyThread.Sleep blocks a OneIM sync threadmedium
ConcurrencyUnbounded loop (While True / Do While True)medium
ConcurrencyShared (static) mutable field — race conditions across sync threadsmedium

Cascade Graph Analysis — 8 Algorithms

The cascade report goes beyond listing nodes. It runs a suite of graph algorithms — each answering a specific question about structural risk in the template dependency web.

AlgorithmQuestion it answers for the team
BFS TraversalWhat are all templates upstream and downstream of this one? How far does the cascade reach?
Cycle DetectionIs there a circular dependency that can never safely evaluate? Where exactly is the loop?
Topological Sort (Kahn's)In what order should templates be evaluated so that no template reads a value that hasn't been written yet?
SCC — Kosaraju's algorithmWhich groups of templates can all reach each other, creating a write-read-write race condition under concurrent sync?
Critical Path (DP)Which chain of templates sets the absolute minimum on total cascade time? Where should optimisation effort go first?
Articulation Points — Tarjan's algorithmWhich single templates, if they fail or are deleted, would disconnect the rest of the cascade? These are structural single points of failure.
Feedback Arc Set (greedy)Which specific dependency relationships should be removed in Designer to break all cycles with the fewest changes?
Dominator Tree — Cooper/Harvey/KennedyWhich templates are mandatory gateways that every evaluation path must pass through? A bug there affects everything downstream.
Betweenness Centrality — Brandes' algorithmWhich templates act as traffic hubs where the most recalculation work converges? These are latency hot-spots under concurrent load.

What It Catches — Practical Examples

RiskWhat the auditor seesConsequence if missed in production
N+1 Database QueryGetListFor() or Create(Session) inside a loopSync that should take 2 seconds takes 20 minutes
Write-cycle deadlockTwo templates that write each other's fields (SCC detection)Sync thread hangs indefinitely; only fix is a server restart
Silent data lossMultiValueProperty.Add() without PutValue writebackChanges appear to succeed but are discarded; no error raised
Swallowed exceptionEmpty Catch blockData corruption happens silently; no log entry, no alert
Hardcoded secretPassword or credential in a connection string literalCredential exposed in Designer export, audit log, version history
Environment mismatchIf col.GetValue = "HR" instead of GetConfigParmValueTemplate breaks when deployed to a different tenant or region
Unguarded FK access.GetFk().GetParent() without .IsEmpty checkNullReferenceException at sync time; sync thread crashes
Schema driftColumn in DEV snapshot missing from PROD snapshotTemplate that works in testing fails silently in production
Performance regressionBenchmark avg rises from 30ms to 65ms vs baselineGradual sync slowdown goes unnoticed until it becomes an incident
SQL injectionString concatenation inside a filter or query expressionAttacker can read or modify any data in the OneIM database

Tool Quality — Self-Testing

The auditor is tested against itself with 410 automated Pester tests across four test lanes. Every one of the 59 rules has a violation.vb and a compliant.vb example file — 118 files total — that serve as living documentation and regression guards.


184
PS 5.1 integration tests
(end-to-end audit runs)
19
PS 7 schema index tests
52
PS 7 benchmark stat tests
(p-values, regression)
155
PS 7 graph algorithm tests
(all 8 algorithms)

How to Run It — Quick Reference

Audit a folder of templates (minimum command)

powershell -NoProfile -File scripts\invoke_oneim_audit.ps1 `
    -TargetPath "C:\OneIM\ExportedTemplates" `
    -ReferenceRepoPath "C:\OneIM\ReferenceRepo"

Audit + benchmark combined report

powershell -NoProfile -File scripts\invoke_oneim_audit.ps1 `
    -TargetPath "C:\OneIM\ExportedTemplates" `
    -ReferenceRepoPath "C:\OneIM\ReferenceRepo" `
    -BenchmarkReportPath "output\benchmark-report.json"

Cascade dependency graph for one template

powershell -NoProfile -File scripts\export_template_cascade_report.ps1 `
    -TemplateName "Person.CentralAccount" `
    -SchemaSnapshotPath "output\schema-snapshot.json"

Run the full test suite

powershell -NoProfile -File scripts\run_auditor_tests.ps1
# Expected: 410 tests, 0 failures, ~80 seconds total

Ideas for Discussion & Potential Extensions

  • CI/CD Pipeline Gate The JSON output and exit codes are already pipeline-ready. A gate step could block a deployment automatically if any new high-severity finding appears compared to the last clean baseline — zero extra tooling needed.
  • Automatic Optimisation Candidates Combine benchmark data + critical path + betweenness centrality: any template that is simultaneously slow, on the critical path, and a high-traffic hub is the highest-ROI target for caching or query optimisation. The tool could surface this list automatically.
  • Cross-Template Config Param Propagation OIM596 already detects hardcoded business values in single files. Extended to the cascade graph, it could identify entire chains where the same hardcoded value flows through multiple templates — flagging all of them at once and suggesting a single shared Configuration Parameter.
  • Designer Live Connection Currently requires a System Debugger export. A read-only Designer API connection would let the tool audit live templates without an export step — enabling continuous background scanning.
  • Scheduled Health Pulse Schema diff + benchmark regression detection already exist as separate tools. A scheduled weekly run combining both could produce a "health pulse" report — a signal the team sees before problems become incidents.
  • Rule Severity Tuning per Project The external audit-config.json already supports overriding detection thresholds. It could be extended to let teams promote or demote rule severities per project context — e.g. a performance-critical project where low-severity performance rules become medium.

OneIM VB.NET Auditor — Internal Tool — Generated 2026-03-31

59 rules • 410 tests • 8 graph algorithms • 0 external dependencies
